title: TDD thin VS Code and Obsidian clients against the Rust broker protocol

do_not_read: other directories in ~/dev unless explicitly referenced

scope: Implement minimal thin host clients. Core implementation remains Rust; TypeScript/JavaScript is allowed only for VS Code and Obsidian extension host glue where those platforms require it.
goals: Use the tdd skill with vertical slices where tests are practical.

Create clients/vscode with InlineCompletionItemProvider host glue that collects prefix/suffix/cursor metadata, debounces requests, cancels stale requests, calls the broker, renders ghost text, and relies on Tab acceptance.
Create clients/obsidian with CodeMirror view plugin host glue that collects note context, debounces requests, cancels stale requests, calls the broker, renders ghost text, and accepts with Tab.
Do not import or reference provider-pi internals from either client.
Share protocol shape through generated schema/fixtures or a tiny generated client artifact from Rust where feasible; avoid duplicating provider logic.
Add tests or static checks that verify request construction and stale-request cancellation behavior for the host glue.
non_goals: Do not implement rich settings UI beyond minimal config.
Do not move provider logic into clients.
safety: Do not add non-Rust core logic beyond unavoidable editor host glue.
Use package manager dependencies only if needed for client build/test scaffolding.
success_criteria: VS Code and Obsidian clients call the same /v1/autocomplete protocol.
Clients include settings for broker URL, debounce, and max context.
Clients cancel stale requests on cursor/document changes.
